result: {"verdict":"ACCEPT","blocking":[],"nonblocking":["ADR scope: docs/decisions/2026-07-08-config-provider-registry.md satisfies the Phase 1 additive config-contract obligation, but its 'Adjacent Track F defaults' section commits circuit-breaker thresholds (failure_threshold: 5, cooldown_seconds: 30, etc.) and decision-logging/redact rules that are outside the config-registry surface and outside Phase 0/Phase 1 config scope. Marked 'accepted' here, these read as pre-deciding Phase 3/4 behavior; recommend demoting to non-normative 'noted, to be ratified in a later ADR' or splitting into a separate ADR to avoid ADR sprawl and premature commitment.","CI trigger coverage: ci.yml only runs on push to main and pull_request targeting main. Pushes to the feature branch phase0-governance-ci produce no check-run; API-readable check-runs will only exist once the PR to main is opened. This is sufficient for the github-pr ledger (PR event creates check-runs on the head SHA under job name 'uv / ruff / pytest', matching the branch-protection open decision), but the verification ledger should explicitly state CI evidence is expected from the PR run, not any feature-branch push.","Python version: setup-python pins '3.11' with no assertion that it matches pyproject requires-python, and uv may provision its own interpreter regardless, making the setup-python step potentially redundant/misleading. Confirm 3.11 matches the project's supported version so CI reproduces the local gate (ruff pass; 17 passed).","CI hygiene: no concurrency group to cancel superseded PR runs and no workflow_dispatch for manual re-runs; optional but cheap hardening for a public repo.","Public-repo hygiene: committing internal method status/verification docs (docs/status, docs/verification) to a public repo is fine (env-var names only, no secret values, protected paths declared), but confirm exposing method/process detail publicly is intended."],"required_patches":[],"certification_statement":"Independent certification of richard-router Phase 0 (branch phase0-governance-ci, base 7bcbf7e). All six Phase 0 obligations are met: (1) .github/workflows/ci.yml runs uv sync --all-groups, uv run ruff check ., and uv run pytest -v on push/PR to main using pinned action tags (checkout@v5, setup-python@v5, setup-uv@v5) and produces API-readable check-runs via the pull_request event; (2) PROJECT.md sets lifecycle mode to github-pr, defines gate commands (ruff, pytest), and points connector scope at the real remote leonbreukelman/richard-router (public, default main); (3) DESIGN-RECORD.md records the mode/CI rationale; (4) the provider-registry ADR documents the additive, backward-compatible Phase 1 config contract; (5) no Phase 1-done overclaim — status and verification docs correctly mark Phase 0 in progress with the PR/CI/merge ledger pending and Phase 1 gated on merge; (6) no secrets, env-var names only. Governance facts are internally consistent with the embedded evidence (HEAD == origin/main == base, ruff pass, 17 passed/1 warning before and after edits). No blocking findings; the noted items are advisory. Verdict: ACCEPT."}
